Why TELTA

Structure you can trust.
People you already know.

01 / SAFETY

Every driver. Every vehicle. Verified.

Roadworthy inspections, PDP checks, and association ID on every windshield. If it carries the TELTA mark, it has been cleared to operate.

02 / GOVERNANCE

An elected leadership, a written code.

TELTA operates under a constitution with an elected Executive Committee, quarterly general meetings, and transparent dispute resolution.

03 / COVERAGE

From Winnie Mandela to Kempton. Covered.

Routes linking every Tembisa section to Kempton Park, Isando, Olifantsfontein, Midrand and Sandton — plus school-run and dedicated hospital trips.

04 / FLEET MANAGEMENT

One association. One standard.

TELTA Fleet Management oversees livery, service records, insurance status, and queue marshal deployment across the network.

05 / COMMUNITY

Have you at heart. Literally.

Our logo is a heart held in two hands — it's not decoration. TELTA runs clean-ups, funeral convoys, learner sponsorships and emergency transport.

06 / PARTNERSHIPS

Affiliated. Aligned. Accountable.

A SANTACO Gauteng affiliate working with Ekurhuleni Metro, the Department of Transport, and private partners on formalisation.

LEADERSHIP · SANTACO engagement /01
Leadership that shows up

On the ground.
In the meetings.
For the rank.

Our executive doesn't run TELTA from behind a desk. You'll find them at the rank at 05:30, in stakeholder meetings at 10:00, and walking the routes you travel every day. That's not a slogan — that's the job.

Meet the Executive

TELTA · Tembisa Taxi Rank Est. 2003 · 23 Years of Service
About TELTA

A taxi association,
yes — but really,
a community that
moves itself.

TELTA was founded by Tembisa operators who believed the township deserved a transport network that worked for commuters first, and one that was answerable to its own people. More than two decades later, that principle still runs the organisation.

Our History

Four moments that made us.

2003 · FOUNDING

Tembisa, for Tembisa

A group of local operators formalise the Tembisa Local Taxi Association with a founding constitution and the first elected executive.

2011 · FORMALISATION

Operating licenses, in order

TELTA coordinates full route-licensing compliance with the Provincial Regulatory Entity — every vehicle, every route, on the books.

2018 · FLEET MGMT

One livery, one standard

Launch of the TELTA Fleet Management programme — branded vehicles, service records, centralised roadworthy audits.

2024 · COMMUNITY

"Have you at heart"

Public adoption of the heart-and-hands mark — signalling TELTA's commitment to community programmes alongside daily operations.

Mission · Vision · Values

What we stand on.

Our Mission

To move Tembisa, safely.

Operating a reliable, affordable, and well-governed minibus taxi service that connects every section of Tembisa to the workplaces, schools, clinics and economic centres of Gauteng.

01
Our Vision

A formalised network, still rooted at home.

To be the benchmark for how a township taxi association operates — professional enough to partner with government and corporates, grounded enough to never forget the commuter at the rank.

02
Our Values

Umphakathi. Uhlonipho. Ukuthembeka.

Community, respect, and accountability. These aren't bullet points on a poster — they are how dispute resolution, driver conduct, and executive decisions are judged internally.

03
Tembisa Local Taxi Rank · Active Where the day begins, every morning at 04:30
Executive Committee
Elected · 2024 – 2027 term

Leadership with a mandate.

Every member of the executive is an operator or long-standing association member, elected by fellow members at the AGM.

M
Chairperson
Head of Association

"Leadership in this space means being at the rank before sunrise, and in the office long after."

D
Deputy Chair
Operations & Routes

"Our routes are our promise. If the taxi is late, we answer for it."

S
Secretary General
Governance & Records

"Everything we do is minuted. That's what separates an association from a rank queue."

T
Treasurer
Finance & Levies

"Every rand has a source and a destination — the members vote on the budget."

C
Compliance Officer
Licenses & Roadworthy

"If a vehicle is not compliant, it does not carry our mark. Non-negotiable."

L
Liaison Officer
Community & Stakeholders

"Residents, Metro, SAPS, schools — we talk to all of them. That is how trust is built."

Our role in the community

More than a ride home.

TELTA members run a road-safety clean-up programme at Tembisa intersections, sponsor learner transport for matric students sitting finals, and provide emergency transport for elderly residents needing to reach Tembisa Hospital.

We also operate a funeral convoy service free of charge for member families — because an association that only cares about fares is not an association worth belonging to.

COMMUNITY · Road cleanup initiative /02
Programmes in Action

Working in the streets, not just at meetings.

Services & Operations

The operating
system of
Tembisa
mobility.

From first-light commuter runs to dedicated school transport and corporate shuttle contracts — here's how the TELTA network actually works, route by route.

What we operate

Services, clearly defined.

SERVICE / 01

Daily commuter transport

Our core service. Scheduled minibus taxi operations on 48 fixed routes, with first departures from 04:30 and last trips returning to Tembisa by 22:00.

Fixed Routes Cash & Card Queue Marshals
SERVICE / 02

Corporate shuttle contracts

Dedicated staff transport for businesses in Isando, Olifantsfontein, Midrand and surrounding industrial nodes. Fixed schedule, branded vehicles, monthly invoicing.

B2B SLA-backed Monthly Billing
SERVICE / 03

School & learner transport

Registered scholar transport on a dedicated fleet — PDP-certified drivers, seat-belt compliance, pick-up and drop-off logged daily.

Scholar Permit Seatbelts Parent SMS
SERVICE / 04

Special trips & charters

Funerals, church trips, stokvel outings, weddings, sports team travel — booked through the association office with a written quote.

Charter Group Bookings Written Quote
SERVICE / 05

TELTA Fleet Management

Our internal programme for members — branded vehicle livery, centralised roadworthy scheduling, insurance verification and driver PDP tracking.

Member Service Livery Audits
SERVICE / 06

Operator support & training

Driver induction, customer-service refreshers, finance and insurance guidance for owners, and formal mediation when disputes arise between operators.

Training Mediation Ops Helpdesk

— FLEET IN OPERATION

600+ vehicles. 48 routes. One association standard.

Safety & Compliance

Safety isn't a slogan.

It's the checklist every member signs, every quarter. Here's what that means on a TELTA-marked vehicle.

The driver side of the protocol.

  • [01]Valid Professional Driving Permit verified annually
  • [02]Drivers induction & customer-service programme completed
  • [03]Zero-alcohol enforcement during operating hours
  • [04]Route-specific training signed off by the Operations Deputy
  • [05]Dispute escalation to the Compliance Officer, logged in writing

The vehicle side of the protocol.

  • [01]Current roadworthy certificate, renewed per regulation
  • [02]Comprehensive passenger insurance in force
  • [03]Quarterly association-level inspection (brakes, tyres, belts)
  • [04]Operating license displayed, matched to the assigned route
  • [05]Fleet livery, association number and emergency contact visible
L
Licensed
Every route and vehicle holds a current operating licence with the PRE.
I
Insured
Passenger liability cover verified annually for every member vehicle.
R
Roadworthy
Association-level quarterly inspections on top of statutory requirements.
A
Accountable
A compliance officer and written grievance procedure, every step logged.
TELTA Fleet Management Branded vehicles · Centralised oversight
FLEET · TELTA branded vehicles /03
For operators

The job of running
a taxi is hard.
Running one alone
is harder.

TELTA membership means access to group insurance rates, centralised licensing renewals, bulk-rate servicing, mediation when conflicts arise, and a seat at the table when route allocations are reviewed.

Latest notices & announcements
18 APR · 2026
R-07 Sandton route: new departure point from 01 May

To ease the Winnie Mandela congestion, the R-07 morning peak queue moves to the secondary loading bay from 1 May 2026. Marshals will be on site to direct commuters.

Active
12 APR · 2026
Quarterly roadworthy audit · members notice

Q2 association roadworthy inspections open on 22 April. All members must book their vehicle inspection slot with the Compliance Officer.

Members
03 APR · 2026
TELTA Youth Road Safety Day · 26 April

Joining Ekurhuleni Metro and local schools for a road-safety awareness programme across three Tembisa intersections. Volunteers welcome.

Community
28 MAR · 2026
Fare schedule review · effective 01 May

Following the fuel-price adjustment, the association has approved a modest fare review on long-haul routes. Local and intra-Tembisa fares remain unchanged.
